posts.title: Hello World
posts.text: Welcome to Hexo! This is your very first post. Check documentation for more info. If you get any problems when using Hexo, you can find the answer in troubleshooting or you can ask me on GitHub. Quick StartCreate a new post1$ hexo new "My New Post" More info: Writing Run server1$ hexo server More info: Server Generate static files1$ hexo generate More info: Generating Deploy to remote sites1$ hexo deploy More info: Deployment

posts.title: Structure From Motion
posts.text: The structure from motion problem in computer vision is the problem of recovering the 3D structure of a stationary scene from a set of projective measurements, represented as a collection of 2D images, via estimation of the motion of the camera corresponding to these images. BackgroundKnowing the 3D structure of real world is meaningful. It’s a generally scientific problem and core technology of a wide variety of fields, such as computer graphics, computer vision, virtual reality, digital media, etc. The structure from motion is one of the most important algorithms to recover 3D structure. It provides a useful process of reconstructing 3D structure from its projections into a series of images taken from different viewpoints. Problem Formulation Input: A collection of 2D images. output: 3D point cloud Assumptions: The scene is static OverviewFor general SfM, it involves the three main stages: Extraction of features in images and matching these features between images. Camera motion estimation. Recovery of the 3D structure using the estimated motion and features. A variety of SfM strategies have been proposed including incremental, hierarchical and glbal approaches. Arguably, incremental SfM is the most popular strategy for reconstruction of unordered photo collections. Incremental SfM commonly starts with feature extraction and matching, followed by geometric verification. The resulting scene graph serves as the foundation for the reconstruction stage, which seeds the model with a carefully selected two-view reconstruction, before incrementally registering new images, triangulating scene points, filtering outliers, and refining the reconstruction using bundle adjustment (BA). Correspondence SearchFeature ExtractionWe wish to the features to be invariant under radiometric and geometric changes so that SfM can uniquely recognize them in multiple images. SIFT, its derivatives, and more recently learned features are the gold standard in terms of robustness. input: A set of images. output:
